div.head: The Secretary of State to the Minister in the Dominican Republic (W. W. Russell)
div.p: Sir: With further reference to the Department’s instruction No. 472 of October 5, 1922, on the question of the Haitian-Dominican boundary, there is transmitted herewith a copy of a further despatch received from General Russell,Gen. Russell’s despatch no. 70, p. 436. together with the Department’s reply thereto.Supra.

You are requested to inform the Dominican Government that General Russell has been instructed to state to the Haitian Government that the Government of the United States expects that the
provisional line established in 1912 shall be scrupulously respected by both the Haitian and Dominican Governments.
The Department hopes to receive, in the very near future, your telegraphic report in reply to its instruction above referred to.
